title: OOF Blend A Tree Model With A Tabular Transformer Specialist
summary: The current search seems dominated by handcrafted GBDT-style heuristics.
rationale: The current search seems dominated by handcrafted GBDT-style heuristics. A contextual tabular transformer can model sparse, high-cardinality categorical interactions like `Driver x Race x Year x Compound x Stint` differently from trees, and the literature reports strong robustness on tabular classification. The key is to use it only as a diversity source in an OOF ensemble, not as a monolithic replacement.
implementation_hint: Train a small TabTransformer/FT-Transformer style model on raw categorical tokens plus normalized numeric inputs, with embeddings for `Driver`, `Race`, `Compound`, `Year`, `Stint`, `Position`. Use strong regularization and early stopping, then blend only OOF predictions with the current best tree model; apply sigmoid calibration if needed to preserve ranking.
expected_effect: Even a weaker standalone model can improve public score if its errors are different from the handcrafted baseline.
risk: Deep tabular models often underperform trees on medium-size tables; if not regularized היט may add noise rather than diversity.
